foundation.title: POINTER
foundation.subtitle: Memory Addresses
foundation.content: A C pointer is just a variable that stores a physical memory address (like 0x7ffd). It doesn't store 'data', it stores 'where data lives'.
foundation.scenario: A Physical Address on an Envelope. The letter (the data) is inside. The address on top (the pointer) tells you exactly which house to go to in the city (RAM).

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Defining a pointer variable using the '*' symbol.

examples.title: Extraction
examples.explanation: Retrieving the literal memory address of a normal variable using '&'.

examples.title: Dereference
examples.explanation: Using the '*' on a pointer to 'open the door' and get the data inside.

foundation.title: MALLOC
foundation.subtitle: Manual Heap
foundation.content: In C, you can ask for RAM yourself from the 'Heap' using malloc(). This memory stays yours until you manually give it back.
foundation.scenario: A Hotel Room. You call (malloc) and ask for a room for 5 people. You can keep the room (RAM) as long as you want until you Check Out (free).

examples.title: The Request
examples.explanation: Asking for 100 bytes of raw memory directly from the operating system.

examples.title: Check In
examples.explanation: Ensuring the system actually HAD memory for you to take.

examples.title: The Null Return
examples.explanation: If there is no more RAM, malloc() returns NULL (A crash in your hands).

foundation.keyPoint: malloc(bytes) returns address of newly allocated RAM.

foundation.title: FREE_MEM
foundation.subtitle: The Check Out
foundation.content: C has no Garbage Collector. If you don't 'free()' the memory you took, your computer slowly runs out of RAM until it crashes.
foundation.scenario: A Book Library. If you check out 1,000 books (malloc) and never return them (free), the library will run out of books and eventually close down (Crash).

examples.title: The Return
examples.explanation: Releasing the address back to the pool so other apps can use it.

examples.title: Dangling Point
examples.explanation: Forgetting to set the pointer to NULL after freeing can cause a crash.

examples.title: Memory Leak
examples.explanation: Losing the address before you free() the memory. Permanent loss.

foundation.keyPoint: Every malloc() must have exactly ONE free().

gameData.hint: Opens the door of the address.
